spec_match.rationale: The page now genuinely probes native XSLTProcessor, runs a real XSLT 1.0 transform when available, and falls back to DOMParser/DOM walking only when removal is active.

interactive.rationale: Three sample inputs (catalog/rss/atom), three render strategies, code panel + output panel. 5 controls.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The feature index promises the same XML rendered by native XSLT, XPath, and DOM walk; all three renderers now produce matching output across catalog, RSS, and Atom samples.

edge_cases.rationale: Covers both sides of the deprecation: real native XSLT execution in browsers that still expose it and a visible removal-state fallback when XSLTProcessor is unavailable.

references.rationale: See-also includes ChromeStatus, the WHATWG removal discussion, Saxon-JS for XSLT 2.0/3.0 migration cases, and FontoXPath for XPath-heavy rewrites.

design_system.rationale: Variables-only styling, neutral letter spacing, no inline handlers, and complete HTML escaping for user-editable XML rendered into the DOM-walk output.

runtime_evidence.rationale: Fresh Chrome DevTools MCP v152 sweep loaded the copied concept, exercised every detected control, found named controls, and reported no post-triage runtime/action failure.

conformance.rationale: A v152 suite now carries the same feature-ID/spec contract without modifying the immutable source-milestone suite.

summary: Resolved the XSLT polyfill explorer critique by replacing the stubbed native renderer with a real XSLTProcessor transform, keeping a clear removal fallback, making XPath namespace-safe for Atom feeds, surfacing the XSL stylesheet beside the JS renderers, and adding migration references for teams that still need XSLT or XPath tooling.
